title: AI-Powered Auto-Mapping of Source to Salesforce Fields via GPT
story: As a user mapping migration fields, I want the system to use GPT to auto-suggest field mappings between source and Salesforce, so that I can save time on obvious matches and focus on ambiguous ones.
description:
Build a GPT-powered auto-mapping function that posts source fields and unmapped Salesforce sink fields to GPT-4 and receives back suggested mappings with confidence-based statuses.

### GPT Prompt Structure
Provide two arrays:
- Source field names with mapped/unmapped indicator
- Salesforce sink fields (only those in "Not Reviewed" status) with data types

### Expected Response (JSON)
- **Sink Field** — only if there is a match
- **Source Field** — as provided
- **Status**:
  - `QA` — exact match with no conflicting alternatives
  - `In Progress` — logical match but multiple possible mappings exist
- **Note** — findings helpful for reviewer when status is "In Progress"

### Goal
Automate obvious field matches to eliminate hundreds/thousands of manual clicks, leaving only ambiguous mappings for human review.
acceptance_criteria: GPT auto-mapping function posts source and sink fields to GPT-4
Response is structured JSON with sink field, source field, status, and note
Exact matches are set to QA status
Ambiguous matches are set to In Progress with reviewer notes
Only unmapped/Not Reviewed Salesforce fields are sent to GPT
Mapped results are applied to the migration mapping UI
ux_changes: New auto-mapping trigger in the migration UI that initiates GPT-powered field matching.
middleware_changes: Backend function to construct the GPT prompt, post to OpenAI API, and parse the JSON response into mapping records.
api_changes: Outbound API call to OpenAI GPT-4 with structured prompt and JSON response parsing.
implementation_notes: Low priority — marked as a future enhancement. The GPT prompt should include the expected JSON structure to ensure consistent responses.
comment_summary: Developer expressed interest in discussing the feature when possible.
